steps.title: Find all three angles in each triangle
steps.content: Angles in a triangle sum to \(180°\). Triangle DEF: \(F = 180° - 47° - 97° = 36°\). Triangle RST: \(T = 180° - 47° - 97° = 36°\). Both triangles have angles \(47°, 97°, 36°\).

steps.title: Apply the AA similarity criterion
steps.content: Two triangles are similar when two pairs of corresponding angles are equal (AA). We are already given \(D = R = 47°\) and \(E = S = 97°\). That is two pairs — AA similarity is already established from the information in the problem statement alone.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• A: The measure of angle F — Angle F can be computed from D and E; it adds nothing needed for similarity. Eliminated.
  • B: The measure of angle T — Angle T can be computed from R and S; it adds nothing needed for similarity. Eliminated.
  • C: The measure of angle F and angle T — Neither is needed since AA is already satisfied. Eliminated.
  • D: No additional information is needed — The two given angle pairs \((D=R=47°, E=S=97°)\) satisfy AA similarity immediately. That matches choice D — D is correct.
